modules.name: pangyplot
modules.intent: Flask entry point. app.py builds the app and loads every chromosome index into memory at startup; routes.py is the whole HTTP surface and delegates to db/query. organisms.py holds the assembly/cytoband config.

modules.name: pangyplot/commands
modules.intent: The `pangyplot <verb>` subcommands -- run, add, annotate, reindex, status. The only place that orchestrates preprocessing end to end; nothing imports it.

modules.name: pangyplot/db
modules.intent: Query orchestration. query.py turns a genomic region into nodes+links by walking the indexes; path_codec.py handles the compressed .binpath format. Routes talk to this, never to sqlite directly.

modules.name: pangyplot/db/indexes
modules.intent: In-memory typed-array indexes loaded at startup from *.quickindex.json.gz caches. Sorted arrays for coordinate->segment lookup; the hot path for every request.

modules.name: pangyplot/db/sqlite
modules.intent: One module per table -- segments, links, paths, steps, bubbles, annotations. Owns all SQL. The indexes read through this; nothing above the index layer should.

modules.name: pangyplot/objects
modules.intent: Plain domain records -- Bubble, Chain, Segment, Link, Path, Annotation. No I/O, no dependencies. The vocabulary everything else is written in.

modules.name: pangyplot/preprocess
modules.intent: Shared preprocessing scaffolding -- run metadata, logging, path setup. The bubble/parser/skeleton/spine stages hang off this.

modules.name: pangyplot/preprocess/bubble
modules.intent: Superbubble detection. Two backends behind PANGYPLOT_FLAT_BUBBLES: the flat int-array pipeline (default, ~half the peak RSS) and the vendored BubbleGun. Both emit a byte-identical bubbles.db.

modules.name: pangyplot/preprocess/parser
modules.intent: Reads the input formats: GFA, odgi layout TSV, GFF3, cytoband. Writes straight into the sqlite tables.

modules.name: pangyplot/preprocess/parser/gfa
modules.intent: The GFA line parsers proper -- segments, links, paths -- split out because GFA parsing is the hot loop of ingest.

modules.name: pangyplot/preprocess/skeleton
modules.intent: Precomputes the low-resolution skeleton and polychain geometry the viewer loads before any detail tiles.

modules.name: pangyplot/preprocess/spine
modules.intent: Builds the reference spine -- the linear backbone the skeleton is laid out against.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js
modules.intent: event-bus.js and app-state.js. Aliased as @event-bus / @app-state in the import map and reachable from every module -- the one intentional global.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph
modules.intent: The viewer's composition root. app.js boots it, state.js holds view state, render-manager.js owns the frame loop and decides skeleton vs detail. Imports nearly everything -- that is its job -- so ignore its fan-out.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/color
modules.intent: Color-by-sample / by-annotation mapping and its state.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/color/legend
modules.intent: The on-canvas color legend.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/data
modules.intent: Fetch layer. Turns /select, /skeleton, /genes responses into records, and holds view-state link resolution -- the backend sends pure GFA s->s links, this resolves them to visual endpoints.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/debug
modules.intent: Debug HUD and status bar. Reaches into everything on purpose; excluded from layering checks.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/debug/views
modules.intent: Debug overlays -- force vectors, gap pressure, hit zones, skeleton grid.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/detail
modules.intent: force-defaults.js -- the default force constants, kept at the top of the detail tree so both engines and UI can read them.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/detail/data
modules.intent: Detail-side data: the pop tree, bubble metadata cache, polychain cache, detail view state.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/detail/data/polychain
modules.intent: Fetches and adapts polychain payloads into the SimObject model.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/detail/engines
modules.intent: The D3 force simulation driver and node hover for the detail view.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/detail/engines/forces
modules.intent: The individual forces -- layout, viewport, anchor-gap, chain-guide, polychain -- plus their tunable settings.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/detail/engines/polychain
modules.intent: Polychain-specific force and hover engines.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/detail/model
modules.intent: The SimObject model -- the heart of the detail view. sim-object.js is the base; BubbleObject / SegmentObject / PolychainContainer are the hierarchy; pop-handler.js runs the pop/unpop lifecycle; segment-registry.js is the id->object map.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/detail/render
modules.intent: Paints the detail view: nodes, links, highlights.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/detail/render/polychain
modules.intent: Paints polychains.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/engines
modules.intent: Interaction engines, coordinated by engine-manager.js: LOD (skeleton<->detail switching), detail transition, keyboard, context menu, node search, reference spine, annotation label drag.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/engines/drag
modules.intent: Node and chain dragging, the centroid anchor, and the (currently disabled) drag-influence system.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/engines/navigation
modules.intent: Pan/zoom and URL-hash navigation.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/engines/path-trace
modules.intent: Haplotype path tracing: decodes the compressed .binpath payload, resolves it onto the current view, renders and animates the traced path.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/engines/selection
modules.intent: Hover, multi-select, and the selection popup.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/render
modules.intent: Shared canvas plumbing: viewport transform, painter utilities, render offset, SVG/PNG export.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/skeleton/data
modules.intent: Skeleton-side data: the precomputed low-res polylines.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/skeleton/engines
modules.intent: Skeleton hover hit-testing.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/skeleton/render
modules.intent: Draws the skeleton view -- base polylines, hover overlay, gene labels and gene polylines.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/ui
modules.intent: The bridge between viewer internals and page chrome -- tooltips, loading state, render/force settings panels, viewport sync, cursor badge.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/graph/utils
modules.intent: geometry.js and frame-scheduler.js. Leaf, imported by 16 modules -- the most depended-on frontend module.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/ui/components
modules.intent: Widgets composed from elements: button-group, tab-set, slider-set, selectable-table.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/ui/elements
modules.intent: Primitive widgets: button, modal, slider, toast, tooltip, search-dropdown. Leaf -- imports nothing.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/ui/sections
modules.intent: The page chrome around the canvas -- navbar, gene search, coordinates, color picker, locale, db selection. Loaded directly by <script type=module> in the templates; talks to the viewer only through the event bus.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/ui/sections/cytoband
modules.intent: Ideogram entry point.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/ui/sections/cytoband/chromosome
modules.intent: Single-chromosome ideogram with the draggable viewport window.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/ui/sections/cytoband/genome
modules.intent: Whole-genome ideogram strip.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/ui/sections/tabs
modules.intent: The tab panels in the sidebar.

modules.name: pangyplot/static/js/utils
modules.intent: Pure helpers -- color and number formatting. Leaf, no imports.

modules.name: pangyplot/utils
modules.intent: Leaf helpers with no project dependencies.
